steps.title: Attach the contrast word
steps.content: The sentence says the designation is hypothetical, then explains that Proto-Indo-European is only postulated. However works as a parenthetical qualifier attached to the first clause before the explanation begins.

steps.title: Place the semicolon
steps.content: Choice A has no punctuation between clauses. Choice B separates the contrast too sharply. Choice C starts the second clause with however, which is less fitting here. Choice D attaches however to designation and uses a semicolon before the explanation. The answer is D.
